title: Exoplanet Transit Photometry Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Exoplanet Transit Photometry Inversion: Exoplanet transit fitting: recover planet radius, orbital parameters, and limb darkening from transit light curves. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.transit.mandel_agol…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~40; stellar_activity_spots, systematics_instrumental set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: F(t) = F_0 * (1 - lambda(p, b, z(t), u1, u2)); p = R_p/R_star, b = a*cos(i)/R_star
E.world_state_x: transit parameters (R_p/R_star, a/R_star, i, t_c, e, omega) + stellar limb darkening (u1, u2)
E.observation_y: normalized flux time series F(t) during transit (N_points epochs)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered transit_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 40); stellar_activity_spots dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Shot poisson sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: sigma(R_p/R_star) < 100 ppm for SNR > 50. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~40. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |stellar_activity_spots|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_points * N_params) per likelihood call per iteration
